title: Some users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft 365 Copilot
impact: Users may have experienced degraded file search results in Copilot.

updates.content:
Title: Some users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)

User impact: Users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot.

More info: In some scenarios, when file search results can’t be retrieved, Copilot may be unable to return relevant responses and instead provide a generic fallback reply.
 
Impacted users may receive the following response:
 
“I’m sorry, I’m not sure how to help with that. Can you try rephrasing?”
 
This behavior can occur when Copilot relies on file-based results, such as content stored in SharePoint, and those results aren’t successfully returned.
 
Not all Copilot interactions are affected, and experiences that don’t depend on file search may continue to work as expected.
 
Current status: We’ve identified that this issue may be related to a recent Copilot service update that changed how agents process knowledge source queries. We’ve disabled a specific component to effectively revert that change in an effort to alleviate this issue. We're reaching out to a subset of impacted users to confirm whether this action restores expected behavior, or if additional actions will need to be taken to fully resolve this impact. 
 
Scope of impact: This incident may impact some users attempting to retrieve file-based responses in Microsoft Copilot. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.
 
Next update by: Sunday, April 12, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)

User impact: Users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot.

More info: In some scenarios, when file search results can’t be retrieved, Copilot may be unable to return relevant responses and instead provide a generic fallback reply.
 
Impacted users may receive the following response:
 
“I’m sorry, I’m not sure how to help with that. Can you try rephrasing?”
 
This behavior can occur when Copilot relies on file-based results, such as content stored in SharePoint, and those results aren’t successfully returned.
 
Not all Copilot interactions are affected, and experiences that don’t depend on file search may continue to work as expected.

Current status: We’ve confirmed that disabling a specific component to revert the offending change successfully resolves the issue for a subset of impacted users. We’re determining the process that we'll take to roll back the change across all impacted environments.

Scope of impact: This incident may impact some users attempting to retrieve file-based responses in Microsoft Copilot. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Next update by: Sunday, April 12, 2026, at 2:00 PM UTC

Start time: Friday, April 10, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)  

User impact: Users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot.  

More info: In some scenarios, when file search results can’t be retrieved, Copilot may be unable to return relevant responses and instead provide a generic fallback reply.
 
Impacted users may receive the following response:
 
“I’m sorry, I’m not sure how to help with that. Can you try rephrasing?”
 
This behavior can occur when Copilot relies on file-based results, such as content stored in SharePoint, and those results aren’t successfully returned.
 
Not all Copilot interactions are affected, and experiences that don’t depend on file search may continue to work as expected.
 
Current status: After further analysis, we've determined that more users are impacted than we initially identified and we've expanded our communications to encompass all potentially affected users and to provide updates on our remediation actions. We've updated the "Scope of impact" to reflect this change. We're monitoring our meticulous rollback of the offending Copilot service update, and we aim to provide a timeline for remediation at the time of our next scheduled update.
 
Scope of impact: This incident may impact some users attempting to retrieve file-based responses in Microsoft Copilot. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.
 
Start time: Friday, April 10, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC
 
Root cause: A recent Copilot service update introduced a regression that's affecting how agents retrieve knowledge from SharePoint sources, which is resulting in impact.
 
Next update by: Sunday, April 12, 2026, at 9:30 PM UTC

Title: Some users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot (Microsoft 365)  

User impact: Users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft Copilot.  

More info: In some scenarios, when file search results can’t be retrieved, Copilot may be unable to return relevant responses and instead provide a generic fallback reply.
Impacted users may receive the following response:

“I’m sorry, I’m not sure how to help with that. Can you try rephrasing?”

This behavior can occur when Copilot relies on file-based results, such as content stored in SharePoint, and those results aren’t successfully returned.

Not all Copilot interactions are affected, and experiences that don’t depend on file search may continue to work as expected.
 
Current status: The rollback of the service update to the last known healthy build is nearing completion with roughly 90 percent completed. We'll continue to monitor this process to ensure it completes as expected and impact is fully resolved.
 
Scope of impact: This incident may impact some users attempting to retrieve file-based responses in Microsoft Copilot. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.
 
Start time: Friday, April 10, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC
 
Root cause: A recent Copilot service update introduced a regression that's affecting how agents retrieve knowledge from SharePoint sources, which is resulting in impact.
 
Next update by: Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at 9:00 AM UTC

Title: Some users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may experience degraded file search results in Copilot.

More info: In some scenarios, when file search results can’t be retrieved, Copilot may be unable to return relevant responses and instead provide a generic fallback reply. Impacted users may receive the following response:

“I’m sorry, I’m not sure how to help with that. Can you try rephrasing?”

This behavior can occur when Copilot relies on file-based results, such as content stored in SharePoint Online, and those results aren’t successfully returned.

Not all Copilot interactions are affected, and experiences that don’t depend on file search may continue to work as expected.

Current status: We’ve confirmed that the rollback deployment is nearly complete across impacted environments. A small portion of the remaining deployment is progressing more slowly than anticipated due to transient deployment failures. We’re continuing to monitor progress and are allowing additional time for deployment retries to complete across environments.

Scope of impact: This incident may impact some users attempting to retrieve file-based responses in Copilot. This information may be updated as our investigation continues.

Start time: Friday, April 10, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC

Root cause: A recent Copilot service update introduced a regression that's affecting how agents retrieve knowledge from SharePoint sources, which is resulting in impact.

Next update by: Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC

This behavior can occur when Copilot relies on file-based results, such as content stored in SharePoint Online, and those results aren’t successfully returned.

Current status: The rollback deployment is taking longer to complete than expected. We anticipate being able to provide a completion timeline by our next scheduled update.

Title: Some users may experience degraded file search results in Microsoft 365 Copilot

User impact: Users may have experienced degraded file search results in Copilot.

More info: In some scenarios, when file search results couldn't be retrieved, Copilot may have provided a generic fallback reply. Impacted users may have received the following response:

“I’m sorry, I’m not sure how to help with that. Can you try rephrasing?”

This behavior could have occurred when Copilot relied on file-based results, such as content stored in SharePoint Online, and those results weren't successfully returned.

Not all Copilot interactions were affected, and experiences that didn't depend on file search may have continued to work as expected.
 
Final status: We’ve completed deployment of the fix across all affected environments and, after monitoring the service, verified that this action successfully resolved the problem.
 
Scope of impact: This incident may have impacted some users attempting to retrieve file-based responses in Copilot.
 
Start time: Friday, April 10, 2026, at 1:00 PM UTC
 
End time: Tuesday, April 14, 2026, at 6:00 AM UTC
 
Root cause: A recent Copilot service update introduced a regression affected how agents retrieve knowledge from SharePoint sources, resulting in impact.
 
Next steps:
- We’re investigating how the recent Copilot service update introduced a regression affecting how agents retrieve knowledge from SharePoint sources to prevent this problem from happening again.
 
This is the final update for the event.
